description: Clearer Information Architecture + Progressive Disclosure + Consistent Navigation polish in the sidebar. Reorganize the flat 7-item nav into calm, scannable logical groups WITHOUT removing any destination. Primary group: Dashboard (Home), Tasks, Calendar. Secondary/de-emphasized group inside a collapsible 'More' section: Planner, Habits, Analytics. Settings pinned at the bottom (above the existing theme/collapse controls). The dashboard/briefing stays the obvious home base. Keep the matte premium theme and existing framer-motion active-indicator behavior. This single feature covers user requirements 1 (IA), 3 (Progressive Disclosure), and 4 (Consistent Navigation & Polish).
steps: In frontend/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx, replace the flat NAV_ITEMS array with grouped structure: PRIMARY = [Dashboard /dashboard, Tasks /dashboard/tasks, Calendar /dashboard/calendar]; SECONDARY (the de-emphasized 'More' group) = [Planner /dashboard/planner, Habits /dashboard/habits, Analytics /dashboard/analytics]; keep Settings /dashboard/settings as a pinned bottom item. Preserve the exact same icons already imported (LayoutDashboard, CheckSquare, Calendar, CalendarClock, Flame, BarChart3, Settings) and the same paths so no destination is removed.

Render the PRIMARY group first with no heading (the core). Render the SECONDARY group under a collapsible 'More' disclosure: a small button row labelled 'More' with a chevron (use ChevronDown/ChevronRight or rotate an existing chevron) that expands/collapses the secondary items with a framer-motion height/opacity transition at 200-300ms. De-emphasize the secondary group visually (e.g. slightly smaller/tertiary-colored 'More' label) while keeping items full-contrast once expanded.

Auto-expand the 'More' section when the current pathname matches one of the secondary routes (so a user already on /dashboard/analytics does not see it hidden). Persist the user's manual expand/collapse choice to localStorage under a new key like 'chronai-sidebar-more-open' (mirror the existing 'chronai-sidebar-collapsed' mounted/localStorage pattern in this file to avoid hydration mismatches).

When the sidebar is in its icon-only collapsed state (collapsed === true), do NOT show the 'More' text toggle; instead render all items (primary + secondary) as icons in order so everything stays reachable, keeping the existing hover tooltips. Settings remains reachable.

Extract the per-item <Link> rendering into a small local helper/sub-component so the active/hover states, the layoutId active indicator ('sidebar-active-indicator'), the active background ('sidebar-active-bg'), min-height 44px, 200ms transitions, and collapsed tooltip are IDENTICAL across primary, secondary, and Settings items. This guarantees uniform, predictable active/hover behavior (requirement 4).
Pin Settings directly above the existing bottom section (theme toggle + collapse toggle) so it reads as a separate utility anchored at the bottom, visually separated from the scrollable nav groups.
Verify the mobile sidebar overlay (sidebarContent is shared) still renders the grouped nav correctly and the More toggle works there too.
Do not change AppShell PAGE_TITLES routes or any page routes. Confirm all 7 destinations (Dashboard, Tasks, Calendar, Planner, Habits, Analytics, Settings) remain navigable.
acceptance_criteria: All 7 nav destinations remain present and navigable: /dashboard, /dashboard/tasks, /dashboard/calendar, /dashboard/planner, /dashboard/habits, /dashboard/analytics, /dashboard/settings.
Primary group (Dashboard, Tasks, Calendar) is shown first; Planner, Habits, Analytics live under a collapsible 'More' section that is collapsed by default for new users.
The 'More' section auto-expands when the active route is one of its items, and its open/closed state persists across reloads via localStorage.
Settings is pinned at the bottom above the theme/collapse controls.
Active and hover states (indicator bar, background glow, color, 200-300ms transitions) are visually identical across all nav items including Settings.
Collapsed (icon-only) sidebar still exposes every destination with tooltips.
`npm run build` and `npm run lint` are clean.
findings: Reorganized frontend/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx only (no other files changed). Replaced the flat NAV_ITEMS array with PRIMARY_ITEMS (Dashboard, Tasks, Calendar), SECONDARY_ITEMS (Planner, Habits, Analytics), and a standalone SETTINGS_ITEM. Same icons/paths preserved, so all 7 destinations remain. Added a de-emphasized collapsible 'More' disclosure (tertiary-colored uppercase label + ChevronRight rotating 0->90deg) wrapping the secondary group with a framer-motion height/opacity transition (duration 0.25s). Collapsed-by-default for new users. Auto-expands via derived moreExpanded = moreOpen || onSecondaryRoute when the active route is a secondary one (without clobbering the persisted manual choice). Manual open/closed persists to localStorage key 'chronai-sidebar-more-open', mirroring the existing 'chronai-sidebar-collapsed' mounted-guard pattern. Extracted a single renderNavItem(item) helper used by primary, secondary, and Settings so the active indicator (layoutId 'sidebar-active-indicator'/'sidebar-active-bg'), background glow, min-h-[44px], 200ms transitions, and collapsed tooltips are identical everywhere. In icon-only collapsed mode the 'More' toggle is hidden and primary+secondary render as icons in order; Settings stays in the bottom section pinned above the theme/collapse controls. sidebarContent is shared so mobile overlay inherits the new grouping. Verification: `npm run lint` clean, `npm run build` compiles successfully, `npm test` 4/4 pass. No page routes or AppShell PAGE_TITLES touched. REVISION (final pass): Fixed the 'More' toggle dead-interaction on secondary routes (review issue 1) - moreOpen is now the single source of truth (moreExpanded = moreOpen) so a manual collapse always wins; the secondary route only auto-expands once on mount via the localStorage/onSecondaryRoute mount effect. Reordered SECONDARY_ITEMS to Habits, Analytics, Planner to match the Sprint 13 brief (review issue 6). Added test coverage for the regrouping (review issue 5): new frontend/__tests__/components/Sidebar.test.tsx mocks next/navigation usePathname/useRouter and theme-provider useTheme and asserts (a) all seven destinations are reachable - Dashboard/Tasks/Calendar/Settings render by default and Planner/Habits/Analytics appear after clicking the 'More' disclosure (queried by link role to avoid framer-motion height flakiness), and (b) the secondary group auto-expands on mount when usePathname is /dashboard/analytics. localStorage cleared in beforeEach. Re-verified: build, lint, and `npm test` (4 suites / 14 tests) all clean.
